g.data-narration: Welcome to Object Assignment in C++. This module covers assignment operators, copy semantics, and memory management.
Object assignment copies one object's data to another using the equals operator. C++ performs member-wise copy by default.
g.text: Object assignment copies data from one object to another using the
assignment operator (=). By default, C++ performs

g.data-narration: Understanding assignment is crucial for memory management. Improper handling causes memory leaks, double-free errors, and dangling pointers.
The assignment operator has a specific signature: ClassName& operator=(const ClassName& obj). It returns a reference for chaining.
Copy constructor creates NEW objects. Assignment operator modifies EXISTING objects. This distinction is critical for exams.
g.text: Box b3 = b1; // NEW object created

g.data-narration: Shallow copy copies pointer ADDRESSES, not actual data. Both objects point to same memory - dangerous!
Deep copy allocates NEW memory and copies actual data. Each object has independent memory - safe!
Here's a simple Complex number class showing default assignment. Both real and imag members are copied directly.
For classes with pointers, implement deep copy assignment. Delete old memory, allocate new, then copy data.
The Rule of Three states: if you need any of destructor, copy constructor, or assignment operator, you likely need all three.
g.text: If you need ONE, you need ALL THREE!

g.data-narration: Always check for self-assignment: if(this != other). Without it, deleting and reallocating memory corrupts data.
Assignment operators return *this enabling chaining: a = b = c. This executes right to left.
Common mistakes: forgetting self-check, shallow copying pointers, and not implementing Rule of Three. These cause exam failures!
In summary: Assignment copies existing objects. Use deep copy for pointers. Follow Rule of Three. Always check self-assignment.
Exam tips: Remember Rule of Three, distinguish copy constructor from assignment, always check self-assignment, return reference for chaining.
g.text: 1. Know when copy constructor vs assignment is called
2. Always implement self-assignment check for deep copy classes
4. Follow Rule of Three for resource-managing classes
